intro: Saccoloma acuminatum is a type of fern, a vascular plant belonging to the family Osmundaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Saccoloma acuminatum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Saccoloma acuminatum is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and it is also used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and ointments to leverage its medicinal benefits.
